profile.name: Alex Dietrich
roles: F/A-18F Super Hornet Pilot, VFA-41 Black Aces
profile.summary: Retired U.S. Navy Lieutenant Commander and F/A-18F Super Hornet pilot who was David Fravor's wingman during the November 14, 2004 Nimitz UAP encounter. Dietrich was in the backseat of a two-seat F/A-18F alongside another pilot when they observed the same Tic Tac-shaped object that Fravor described. Where Fravor dove toward the object and attempted to intercept it, Dietrich maintained altitude as the designated observer - making her account the single most important corroborating witness to Fravor's encounter. She kept her account private for over a decade before going public in a May 2021 60 Minutes interview, making her the first female military UAP witness to come forward publicly in the modern disclosure era.

key_events.event: Commissioned as a Naval Aviator; assigned to VFA-41 Black Aces flying F/A-18F Super Hornets

Participates in the Nimitz UAP encounter off the coast of San Diego. Flying as the wingman to David Fravor's flight lead, observes the Tic Tac-shaped object while maintaining altitude as the designated observer. Sees the object accelerate away when Fravor closes on it. Describes a sense of 'eerie' discomfort and a feeling that she was being observed in return.
Keeps her account of the encounter private for approximately 17 years, consistent with an informal understanding among the pilots that discussing the encounter was professionally inadvisable

Goes public for the first time in a CBS 60 Minutes segment alongside David Fravor. Describes the encounter in detail, including her emotional response and the object's behavior. Her account matches Fravor's in all material details and adds the witness perspective of the maintaining-altitude observer.

associated_people.name: David Fravor
associated_people.role: Flight lead; Nimitz encounter primary witness
associated_people.relationship: Dietrich was Fravor's wingman during the Nimitz encounter. Where Fravor engaged the object directly, Dietrich maintained altitude as observer. Her account is the primary independent corroboration of Fravor's encounter.

disclosures.title: 60 Minutes - UAP Segment
disclosures.notes: First public account of the Nimitz encounter from Dietrich's perspective. Appeared alongside Fravor, Ryan Graves, and others. Described her emotional experience and the object's behavior as the maintaining-altitude observer.

sources.title: CBS 60 Minutes - UAP Segment (2021)
sources.notes: Dietrich's first and most detailed public account of the Nimitz encounter

sources.title: Wikipedia - Tic Tac UAP Incident
sources.notes: Documents the Nimitz encounter including Dietrich's role as wingman observer

supporting: Retired U.S. Navy F/A-18F pilot with verifiable military service record and direct operational experience relevant to the event she describes
Was a first-hand visual witness to the Tic-Tac object alongside Cmdr. Fravor - independent corroboration of the primary witness account

Her account is consistent with Fravor's in key details while also noting her distinct perspective (she maintained altitude rather than descending), demonstrating independent memory rather than rehearsed narrative
Coming forward publicly on 60 Minutes in 2021 represented significant personal exposure for a recently retired officer
Her measured, factual description of the encounter avoids speculation about origin or nature, consistent with professional credibility standards
contradicting: She was in a different position during the encounter than Fravor, limiting her direct observation of the object's most anomalous maneuvers
The video evidence from the encounter (FLIR footage) does not definitively corroborate the specific visual characteristics she and Fravor describe
Like all Nimitz witnesses, she has no direct knowledge of what the object was or where it came from - her testimony establishes an anomalous observation, not its cause
Her long delay in going public (17 years) means there is a longer memory-reliability gap between the event and her recorded testimony than is ideal

career_connections.relationship: Dietrich was Fravor's wingman during the Nimitz encounter; her account from the maintaining-altitude observer position is the primary corroboration of Fravor's engagement with the Tic Tac object
